channel.title: Darkhorse Press - Top Story
channel.description: Latest Top Story posts from Darkhorse Press.

item.title: Hattiesburg police seek person of interest in commercial burglary investigations - Darkhorse Press
item.author: Morgan Howard
item.description:
HATTIESBURG – The Hattiesburg Police Department is asking for the public’s help locating a person of interest in connection with multiple commercial burglary investigations.

According to police, the incidents occurred in the 100 block of Industrial Drive on Dec. 14, 2025, Jan. 18, 2026, and Feb. 15, 2026. Source: Hattiesburg PD

Detectives are seeking to speak with Eddie McCormick, 27, of Laurel, regarding the incidents.

Authorities are asking anyone with information about McCormick’s whereabouts or details related to the burglaries to contact the Hattiesburg Police Department.

Anonymous tips can also be submitted through Crime Stoppers by calling or texting **TIPS. Tips may also be submitted online at www.P3Tips.com .

item.title: Vicksburg police seek public’s help locating woman wanted for embezzlement - Darkhorse Press
item.author: Morgan Howard
item.description:
VICKSBURG – The Vicksburg Police Department is asking for the public’s help locating a woman wanted in connection with an embezzlement case.

Police said Princess Grissom-Carter is wanted for embezzlement. Her date of birth is Aug. 11, 1981.

Anyone with information about Grissom-Carter’s whereabouts is asked to contact the Vicksburg Police Department at 601-636-2511 or Investigator Gladys Kersh at 601-876-8045.

item.author: Morgan Howard
item.description:
VICKSBURG – The Vicksburg Police Department is asking for the public’s help locating a man wanted in connection with a credit card fraud case.

Police said Mark Anthony Miller II is wanted for credit card fraud. His date of birth is Feb. 14, 1980.

Anyone with information about Miller’s whereabouts is asked to contact the Vicksburg Police Department at 601-636-2511 or Investigator Barfield at 601-876-8494.

item.title: Trailer destroyed in fire on Otis Lee Road in Macedonia - Darkhorse Press
item.author: Morgan Howard
item.description:
MACEDONIA – Firefighters with the Macedonia Volunteer Fire Department responded Wednesday afternoon to a fire that destroyed a single-wide trailer on Otis Lee Road.

Crews were dispatched to the 100 block of Otis Lee Road at approximately 5:30 p.m. on March 4 after receiving a report of a trailer fire.

Upon arrival, firefighters found flames visible from multiple sides of the structure, with the fire rapidly spreading throughout the trailer, according to the department. Crews deployed hose lines from the first arriving engine and began an interior attack.

Firefighters later transitioned to a defensive attack after structural instability and collapse occurred inside the trailer.

Crews remained on scene for about two hours to bring the fire under control and extinguish hot spots. The trailer was unoccupied at the time of the fire, and no injuries were reported.

After speaking with the property owner, officials determined the fire began while leaves were being burned in the yard. The fire spread out of control and eventually ignited the trailer, the department said.

North Forrest Volunteer Fire Department and Dixie Electric Power Association also responded to assist.

The Macedonia Volunteer Fire Department reminded residents that outdoor burning can quickly become dangerous, especially during dry or windy conditions. Officials urged residents to keep burn piles a safe distance from structures and to have a water source or other means of controlling the fire available before starting an outdoor burn.

item.title: State Auditor’s Office arrests former Laurel public works director - Darkhorse Press
item.author: Morgan Howard
item.description:
JACKSON – State Auditor Shad White announced Thursday that special agents with his office have arrested a former Laurel public works director accused of misusing city equipment and attempting to conceal evidence related to the investigation.

According to the Mississippi Office of the State Auditor, Luther Russell, the former Laurel Public Works director, is accused of using heavy equipment owned by the City of Laurel to perform work on his personal property.

Investigators allege Russell instructed employees to load the city’s track hoe onto a city-owned low-boy trailer and transport the equipment to his property, where it was allegedly used for personal work.

Authorities also said the roadway outside Russell’s home was damaged when the heavy equipment was transported to the property. After the county instructed that the roadway be repaired, Russell allegedly ordered employees to disable the GPS tracking device on a city-owned vehicle to conceal the vehicle’s movements while repairing the road.

“Thank you to the agents in my office who did the hard work in uncovering this scheme,” White said in a statement. “We will continue to work closely with prosecutors to get record results for taxpayers.”

Russell faces up to $8,000 in fines and up to 30 years in prison if convicted.

Officials noted that all persons arrested by the Mississippi Office of the State Auditor are presumed innocent until proven guilty in a court of law.

The State Auditor’s Office encourages the public to report suspected fraud by visiting the auditor’s website and clicking the reporting link or by calling 1-800-321-1275 during normal business hours.

item.title: Pike County man gets 30 years for aggravated cocaine trafficking - Darkhorse Press
item.author: Therese Apel
item.description:
PIKE COUNTY — A Pike County jury found Charles Pittman guilty of aggravated trafficking of cocaine following a trial in Pike County Circuit Court on Tuesday.

Circuit Judge Michael M. Taylor sentenced Pittman to 30 years in the custody of the Mississippi Department of Corrections. Under Mississippi’s aggravated trafficking statute, the sentence must be served day-for-day without eligibility for early release.

Prosecutors said evidence at trial showed Pittman and a co-defendant who previously pleaded guilty traveled to Texas to obtain cocaine and transport it back to Pike County for distribution.

The case stemmed from a joint investigation by the Mississippi Bureau of Narcotics, Pike County Sheriff’s Department, Mississippi Highway Patrol and the Mississippi Forensics Laboratory.

The case was prosecuted by Assistant District Attorney Sebastian Harrell.

District Attorney Adams praised the officers and jurors involved in the case.

“This conviction demonstrates the commitment of law enforcement and prosecutors across our district to seek justice and accountability as they aggressively combat drug trafficking,” Adams said in a statement. “Let this serve as a clear message to drug dealers: we will continue to work together, one case at a time, to stop drugs from being brought into and distributed within the 14th Circuit Court District.”

The Office of the District Attorney also thanked the jurors who served and the officers who participated in the investigation, citing their dedication to community safety.

item.title: No injuries reported after train collides with vehicle on Highway 49 in Yazoo County - Darkhorse Press
item.author: Morgan Howard
item.description:
YAZOO COUNTY – Deputies responded Thursday morning to a collision involving a train and a vehicle on Highway 49, according to authorities.

At approximately 11:30 a.m. on March 5, deputies were dispatched to the area of Highway 49 near Coker Road after receiving reports of a train striking a silver vehicle.

When deputies arrived, they found that there were no injuries reported. Officials said the train had passengers onboard at the time of the incident, and there was one person inside the silver vehicle.

Authorities said the crash resulted in minimal damage, and everyone involved was unharmed.

The incident remains under investigation.

item.title: Man Hospitalized With Life-Threatening Injuries After Tupelo Apartment Shooting - Darkhorse Press
item.author: Therese Apel
item.description:
TUPELO — A man was hospitalized with life-threatening injuries Wednesday night after a shooting at a Tupelo apartment complex, authorities said.

Officers with the Tupelo Police Department responded around 9:40 p.m. March 4 to Monarch Creek Apartments, located at 1321 Ida B. Wells St., in reference to a shooting.

When officers arrived, they found an adult male suffering from multiple gunshot wounds. The victim was transported to North Mississippi Medical Center for treatment.

Investigators also discovered multiple vehicles at the scene that had been struck by gunfire.

No arrests have been made, and the case remains under active investigation.

Anyone with information is asked to contact the Tupelo Police Department at 662-841-6491 or Crime Stoppers of Northeast Mississippi at 800-773-TIPS (8477).

item.title: Man gets $2 million bond in Jackson double murder case - Darkhorse Press
item.author: Therese Apel
item.description:
JACKSON — A man has been charged with two counts of murder in connection with a Sunday night shooting that left two people dead in Jackson.

Michael Knight appeared Tuesday in Municipal Court, where bond was set at $2 million for two murder charges. Authorities allege Knight is responsible for the shooting deaths of Antonio Arnold and Glen McWilliams.

Officers with the Jackson Police Department were dispatched around 10 p.m. Sunday to the 5300 block of Dogwood Trail near Oakridge Road in reference to a shooting.

Upon arrival, officers found two deceased individuals: a Black male lying in a ditch and a white male lying in the street nearby.

The victims were identified as 31-year-old Antonio Arnold and 63-year-old Glen McWilliams. Authorities said next of kin have been notified.

The department’s Robbery/Homicide Unit and Mobile Crime Unit responded to the scene, along with the Hinds County Coroner’s Office.

The investigation remains active.

Anyone with information about the case is asked to contact the Jackson Police Department at 601-960-1234 or Crime Stoppers at 601-355-TIPS (8477).

item.title: JSU announces the passing of former acting president Dr. Elayne Hayes-Anthony - Darkhorse Press
item.author: Special to Darkhorse Press
item.description:
JACKSON — The Jackson State University community is mourning the loss of Elayne Hayes-Anthony, Ph.D., a respected educator, communications leader, and former acting president of the university, who passed away this morning, March 5, 2026.

Hayes-Anthony dedicated decades of service to advancing education, journalism and public communication in Mississippi and beyond. Known for her unwavering commitment to student success and academic excellence, she mentored countless aspiring journalists and communications professionals while helping strengthen JSU’s legacy as a leading historically Black university.

“As we mourn the passing of Dr. Elayne Hayes-Anthony, we remember her career as she remained deeply committed to the success of our students and to the advancement of journalism and media education at Jackson State,” said Interim President Denise Jones-Gregory, Ph.D. “Her leadership in the classroom, within her department and across the institution helped shape generations of communicators and storytellers. On behalf of the Jackson State University family, we extend our sincere condolences to her family, former students and colleagues.”

A proud alumna of Jackson State, Hayes-Anthony earned both her bachelor’s degree in speech communication with an emphasis in broadcasting and a master’s degree in educational technology from the university. She later earned a doctorate in organizational communication and broadcast law from Southern Illinois University Carbondale.

Throughout her distinguished career, Hayes-Anthony held numerous leadership roles in higher education and public service. She served as professor and chair of the Department of Journalism and Media Studies at Jackson State University, prior to her death, and previously chaired the Department of Communications at Belhaven University for 17 years.

In 2023, Hayes-Anthony was appointed acting president of Jackson State University, guiding the institution during a pivotal period and continuing her long-standing commitment to the university’s mission of educating and empowering future leaders.

Before her work in higher education administration, Hayes-Anthony built a groundbreaking career in broadcast journalism. She made history as the first Black woman to serve as an anchor, producer and reporter at WJTV Channel 12 in Jackson, Mississippi, helping open doors for women and people of color in the news industry.

Her influence extended beyond the classroom and newsroom. Hayes-Anthony served as president of the Mississippi Association of Broadcasters, becoming the first Black woman and journalism educator to lead the organization. She also served as assistant superintendent of communications for Jackson Public Schools and held numerous civic and professional leadership roles throughout the state.

Hayes-Anthony was widely admired for her passion for teaching, her commitment to community engagement, and her tireless advocacy for journalism and ethical communication.

She is survived by her husband, Eddie Anthony; her son, Willard Anthony; and her grandson, Aiden Anthony. Funeral arrangements will be announced by the family at a later date.

item.title: Jackson Police Seek Public’s Help Identifying Armed Robbery Suspect - Darkhorse Press
item.author: Therese Apel
item.description:
JACKSON — The Jackson Police Department is asking for the public’s help in identifying a suspect in an armed robbery that occurred last year.

The robbery took place Oct. 16, 2025, in the 2100 block of Thousand Oaks Drive, according to police. Authorities did not release additional details about the suspect or the circumstances surrounding the incident.

Investigators are urging anyone with information related to the case to come forward.

Anyone with information is asked to contact the Jackson Police Department at 601-960-1234 or Crime Stoppers at 601-355-TIPS (8477).

item.title: Forrest County Sheriff’s Office Evaluation Center certified as mental health holding facility - Darkhorse Press
item.author: Morgan Howard
item.description:
FORREST COUNTY – The Forrest County Sheriff’s Office Evaluation Center has been certified as a Designated Mental Health Holding Facility, according to the sheriff’s office.

The facility, sometimes referred to as “The Annex,” is one of only two locations in Mississippi to receive the designation.

Officials said the certification allows the center to temporarily house individuals who are legally required to receive mental health treatment through involuntary commitment but are waiting for placement at a long-term treatment facility.

The centers serve as secure locations where individuals can stay while awaiting transportation to a permanent treatment center.

While individuals are waiting for placement, the facilities must provide basic care and services. According to the sheriff’s office, this includes a place to sleep, food, medical care and access to necessary medications. Medical staff are also responsible for evaluating the person’s condition to determine whether intensive hospital care is needed or if a less restrictive environment may be appropriate.

Sheriff Charlie Sims said the certification reflects the dedication of the center’s staff and their commitment to providing care to those in crisis.

“The certification of the Forrest County Sheriff’s Office Evaluation Center as a Designated Mental Health Holding Facility represents a major step forward for our county and for the state of Mississippi,” Sims said in a statement. “Being one of only two facilities in the state to achieve this designation reflects our commitment to doing this work the right way. I am extremely proud of the men and women who work at the Evaluation Center. Their dedication, professionalism and compassion demonstrate that they truly care about the citizens entrusted to us during some of the most vulnerable moments of their lives.”

Officials said staff at the Evaluation Center worked extensively to meet the requirements necessary to achieve the certification.

item.title: UPDATE: Brandon woman killed after hit by car while riding bike in Jackson - Darkhorse Press
item.author: Morgan Howard
item.description:
JACKSON – A woman is dead after she was hit by a car while riding a bike in Jackson.

According to police, the crash happened in the 4800 block of Chastain Drive, vehicle vs bicycle. Two individuals were riding on a bicycle when they were struck by a vehicle.

There is no known description of the vehicle. The man who was also on the bicycle was transported by AMR to a local hospital to be treated for his injuries.

Jackson Coroner Jeramiah Howard identified the victim as 21-year-old Kaitlyn Mangum Gast, from Brandon.

Police were called at 11:11 this morning to the scene.

item.title: Clinton unveils Mississippi Blues Trail marker honoring historic Grits & Gravy Recording Studio - Darkhorse Press
item.author: Morgan Howard
item.description:
CLINTON – The City of Clinton unveiled its second Mississippi Blues Trail marker Thursday, recognizing the legacy of the historic Grits & Gravy Recording Studio and its role in Mississippi’s musical history.

The studio, once located inside the former Hilltop Theatre, was founded in the mid-1960s by Bob McRee and brothers Cliff and Ed Thomas. During its years of operation, the studio attracted several notable artists, including Dorothy Moore, Junior Parker, Barbara Lynn, Freddy Fender and the GRAMMY-nominated duo Peggy Scott & Jo Jo Benson.

City officials said the studio helped leave a lasting mark on the state’s music scene.

After the recording studio era ended, Mississippi College continued the building’s musical tradition by using the space as the Choctaw Band Hall. The building remained a place for music until it was eventually demolished in 2013.

The unveiling ceremony was hosted in partnership with Visit Mississippi, the Mississippi Blues Commission and Mississippi College. Following the ceremony, Mississippi College provided refreshments for attendees, including grits and gravy, in tribute to the studio’s name and legacy.

Although the studio building no longer stands, officials said its history will now be preserved through the Mississippi Blues Trail marker.

With the addition of the marker, Clinton joins more than 200 locations across the state recognized on the Mississippi Blues Trail, a statewide initiative that honors the people, places and moments that helped shape Mississippi’s reputation as the birthplace of America’s music.

item.title: Clinton Fire Department seeks donations to restore historic 1964 Mack fire engine - Darkhorse Press
item.author: Morgan Howard
item.description:
The Clinton Fire Department is asking for the public’s support to help restore a historic 1964 Mack fire engine that could become a community parade truck and piece of local memorabilia.

Officials said the antique fire truck, which has been properly stored and is still somewhat drivable, recently traveled between Station 1 and Station 4 across town. The vehicle has also been featured as part of Station 1’s Christmas decorations in the past.

While the truck is no longer suitable for firefighting, the department said it has the potential to serve as a parade vehicle and a reminder of Clinton’s firefighting history if restored.

The request for donations comes after the department recently placed a 1955 Chevy fire truck up for sale. Fire officials said the decision was not made lightly but was necessary due to space limitations and financial priorities.

According to the department, restoring the 1955 Chevy could have cost between $50,000 and $100,000. Officials said it would have been fiscally irresponsible to spend that amount on a vehicle that could not serve an operational role.

The department also noted that it faces other financial needs, including competitive firefighter salaries, updated equipment and the eventual purchase of new fire engines. New fire apparatus can cost between $750,000 and $2.5 million each, according to officials.

After hearing concerns from residents about the sale of the 1955 Chevy truck, the department said the community now has an opportunity to participate in preserving a different piece of Clinton’s fire service history by contributing to the restoration of the 1964 Mack.

The restoration project includes work on the truck’s fuel system, clutch and brake systems, as well as restoring the wooden hose bed and improving the vehicle’s exterior finish. Planned repairs include refurbishing the fuel tank, inspecting fuel lines, rebuilding brake components, replacing deteriorated wood with oak planks and restoring the truck’s appearance through paint touch-ups or polishing.

Fire officials said community donations would help cover the cost of these repairs and bring the historic engine closer to its original 1964 condition.

The Clinton Fire Department said it appreciates the support it receives from residents throughout the year and hopes the community will join in helping restore the historic vehicle.
item.ns1:encoded:
The Clinton Fire Department is asking for the public’s support to help restore a historic 1964 Mack fire engine that could become a community parade truck and piece of local memorabilia.

Officials said the antique fire truck, which has been properly stored and is still somewhat drivable, recently traveled between Station 1 and Station 4 across town. The vehicle has also been featured as part of Station 1’s Christmas decorations in the past.

While the truck is no longer suitable for firefighting, the department said it has the potential to serve as a parade vehicle and a reminder of Clinton’s firefighting history if restored.

The request for donations comes after the department recently placed a 1955 Chevy fire truck up for sale. Fire officials said the decision was not made lightly but was necessary due to space limitations and financial priorities.

According to the department, restoring the 1955 Chevy could have cost between $50,000 and $100,000. Officials said it would have been fiscally irresponsible to spend that amount on a vehicle that could not serve an operational role.

The department also noted that it faces other financial needs, including competitive firefighter salaries, updated equipment and the eventual purchase of new fire engines. New fire apparatus can cost between $750,000 and $2.5 million each, according to officials.

After hearing concerns from residents about the sale of the 1955 Chevy truck, the department said the community now has an opportunity to participate in preserving a different piece of Clinton’s fire service history by contributing to the restoration of the 1964 Mack.

The restoration project includes work on the truck’s fuel system, clutch and brake systems, as well as restoring the wooden hose bed and improving the vehicle’s exterior finish. Planned repairs include refurbishing the fuel tank, inspecting fuel lines, rebuilding brake components, replacing deteriorated wood with oak planks and restoring the truck’s appearance through paint touch-ups or polishing.

Fire officials said community donations would help cover the cost of these repairs and bring the historic engine closer to its original 1964 condition.

The Clinton Fire Department said it appreciates the support it receives from residents throughout the year and hopes the community will join in helping restore the historic vehicle.

item.title: Charges upgraded to attempted murder in Gulf Park Estates shooting - Darkhorse Press
item.author: Therese Apel
item.description:
JACKSON COUNTY — Two men initially charged with aggravated assault in a late February shooting in Gulf Park Estates are now facing upgraded charges of attempted murder, authorities said.

According to the Jackson County Sheriff’s Department, Christopher Paul Landry, 46, and Glen Guy Barton, 32, were formally charged with attempted murder following developments in the investigation of a Feb. 26 shooting near North 19th Street and Pickering Forrest Drive.

Sheriff John Ledbetter said deputies responded around 9:30 p.m. to reports of a shooting and found 47-year-old Nathan Lee suffering from two gunshot wounds after an altercation with another party. Lee was transported to a local hospital and is listed in stable condition.

Barton was initially arrested without incident and charged with one count of aggravated assault with a deadly weapon. Landry was later located and arrested on Derry Lane in Ocean Springs after the sheriff’s department received a Crime Stoppers tip regarding his whereabouts. He was taken into custody on an outstanding warrant for aggravated assault with a deadly weapon.

As the investigation progressed, authorities upgraded the charges against both men to attempted murder. Barton also faces additional charges of possession of a weapon by a convicted felon and tampering with physical evidence. Officials said Barton was out on bond for a previous felony charge at the time of his arrest.

On March 4, Barton and Landry made their initial appearances before Mark Watts in Jackson County Court. Both men are being held at the Jackson County Sheriff’s Department Adult Detention Center.

The case remains under active investigation, and additional charges or arrests may be forthcoming.

Anyone with information is urged to contact the Jackson County Sheriff’s Office at 228-769-3063 or Crime Stoppers at 877-787-5898.

item.title: Adams County most wanted suspect turns herself in - Darkhorse Press
item.author: Morgan Howard
item.description:
ADAMS COUNTY – One of Adams County’s Most Wanted individuals turned herself in to authorities Monday afternoon, according to the Adams County Sheriff’s Office.

Terrinika L. White, 33, of Natchez, surrendered at the Adams County Sheriff’s Office on March 2 at approximately 2:30 p.m.

White was taken into custody on an indictment and processed into the Adams County Jail. Authorities said she was later released after posting a $5,000 bond.

According to the sheriff’s office, White faces charges that include false pretense, simple assault on a law enforcement officer and felony fleeing.

White is scheduled to make her initial court appearance March 5 at 9 a.m. in Adams County Circuit Court.

Sheriff Travis Patten encouraged individuals with outstanding warrants to turn themselves in and address their legal matters through the court system.

The Adams County Sheriff’s Office said it remains committed to locating and apprehending individuals wanted for criminal charges in the community.

item.title: Vehicle pursuit in Forrest County ends with two arrests - Darkhorse Press
item.author: Morgan Howard
item.description:
FORREST COUNTY – A traffic stop attempt Tuesday evening in Forrest County turned into a vehicle pursuit that ended with two arrests, according to the Forrest County Sheriff’s Office.

Deputies tried to stop a vehicle around 6 p.m. March 3, 2026, after suspecting it was involved in criminal activity, the sheriff’s office said. The driver refused to stop and fled, prompting a pursuit.

With help from the Lamar County Sheriff’s Office and the Hattiesburg Police Department, authorities stopped the vehicle and took the occupants into custody without incident, the sheriff’s office said.

The driver, Shaquille Catrell Adams, was arrested and charged with felony eluding law enforcement, possession of a controlled substance and reckless driving, according to the sheriff’s office.

A passenger, Tearra Gray, was arrested and charged with disorderly conduct for failure to comply with law enforcement commands, the sheriff’s office said.

Both were transported to the Forrest County Jail.

Sheriff’s officials said Adams has multiple prior felony arrests and is currently on probation with the Mississippi Department of Corrections. MDOC has been notified, and a probation revocation may be pursued.

The sheriff’s office said the investigation remains ongoing and thanked assisting agencies for their help.

item.title: UPDATE: Missing man’s vehicle found abandoned near Natchez riverfront - Darkhorse Press
item.author: Morgan Howard
item.description:
ADAMS COUNTY – Search efforts are continuing in Adams County for a 31-year-old man who was reported missing last month, according to the Adams County Sheriff’s Office.

Silver Alert issued for missing Covington County man

Cornelius Elijah Allen was originally reported missing out of Covington County on Feb. 25. Authorities said Allen was believed to be traveling in a 2017 black Volkswagen Tiguan with Mississippi license plate 910 CVB.

On Feb. 26, the vehicle was located abandoned near the intersection of D.A. Biglane Road and Canal Street in Natchez. The Natchez Police Department towed the vehicle from the scene.

Investigators said additional evidence connected to the case was discovered the following day. On Feb. 27, Allen’s car keys were found along the bank of the Mississippi River near the Natchez port area.

The Natchez Police Department is leading the investigation, with assistance from the Adams County Sheriff’s Office and other partner agencies.

Search and rescue teams launched operations in the Mississippi River on March 3, searching until approximately 4:30 p.m. The Mississippi Department of Wildlife, Fisheries and Parks has also assisted with the search alongside local law enforcement agencies.

Search operations resumed Wednesday at approximately 9 a.m., with teams continuing to search along the Mississippi River and nearby wooded areas.

Authorities are asking the public for assistance in reviewing any available security or camera footage from the day Allen’s vehicle was believed to be in the area. Investigators are specifically requesting residents and businesses check footage recorded Feb. 25 between 4:51 a.m. and 1:46 p.m.

Officials said cameras located near D.A. Biglane Road, Canal Street, the Natchez Port area and surrounding riverfront locations may have captured information that could help investigators establish a more accurate timeline of Allen’s movements.

Anyone who finds anything unusual or relevant on camera footage is asked to contact the Adams County Sheriff’s Office at 601-442-2752 or the Natchez Police Department at 601-445-5565. Those wishing to remain anonymous may contact Southwest Regional Crime Stoppers at 888-442-5001.

The Adams County Sheriff’s Office and the Natchez Police Department said they appreciate the continued support and cooperation from the community as search efforts remain ongoing.

item.title: Two sentenced in $18K theft from elderly victim in Rankin County - Darkhorse Press
item.author: Morgan Howard
item.description:
RANKIN COUNTY – Mississippi Attorney General Lynn Fitch announced the sentencing of Byron Gage Tatum, 26, and Chesney Lynn Stanley, 23, after the two pleaded guilty to one count each of grand larceny in a case involving an elderly victim.

Fitch’s office said Tatum and Stanley stole nearly $20,000 from the victim. The case was investigated and prosecuted by the Mississippi Attorney General’s Office.

“Preying on senior citizens, who need and deserve our extra care and protection, will not be tolerated,” Fitch said in a statement. “I am proud of the diligent work of our investigators and prosecutors who ensured that justice was served in this case.”

On Feb. 24,, Rankin County Circuit Court Judge Dewey Arthur sentenced both Tatum and Stanley to 10 years in the custody of the Mississippi Department of Corrections, with one year to serve. The remaining time was suspended, with five years of supervised release and four years of unsupervised probation.

The two were ordered to pay $18,570.40 in restitution and $500 to the Crime Victim Compensation Fund, according to the attorney general’s office.

item.title: Two Booneville men charged in Prentiss County shed burglary - Darkhorse Press
item.author: Morgan Howard
item.description:
PRENTISS COUNTY – Two Booneville men have been charged following a burglary investigation in Prentiss County, according to the Prentiss County Sheriff’s Department.

Deputies said the investigation began Feb. 6, 2026, after authorities received a report that a shed on County Road 1219 had been broken into. Several items were reported stolen from the structure.

During the investigation, authorities identified two suspects on Feb. 14 as Dustin Blade Asher, 23, and Austin Tyler Adams, 30, both of Booneville.

Both men were charged with burglary of a commercial building.

Adams also faces additional charges of possession of a weapon by a felon and conspiracy, and is being charged as a habitual offender.

Bond for Asher was set at $25,000, while Adams’ bond was set at $105,000.

The case will be presented to the next Prentiss County Grand Jury by Investigator Aaron Stolz. Investigators Torie Jumper and Kolby Knight assisted with the case.

item.title: Ridgeland police seek help identifying man wanted for questioning in bank scam - Darkhorse Press
item.author: Morgan Howard
item.description:
RIDGELAND – The Ridgeland Police Department is asking for the public’s help identifying an unknown man wanted for questioning in connection with a bank scam involving a local resident.

Police said the incident occurred in late January 2026. The man is described as wearing a dark-colored hoodie with “Golden State Warriors” embroidered on the front.

Investigators asked anyone who recognizes the man or has information that could help identify him to contact the Ridgeland Police Department Criminal Investigations Division at 601-856-2121.

Tips may also be reported to Central Mississippi Crime Stoppers at 601-355-TIPS (8477) or through the organization’s website at www.centralmscrimestoppers.com .

item.title: Police pursuit ends in crash at Columbia and North streets in Hattiesburg - Darkhorse Press
item.author: Morgan Howard
item.description:
HATTIESBURG – A police pursuit Monday evening ended in a crash involving two vehicles at a Hattiesburg intersection, according to the Hattiesburg Police Department.

Officers said they attempted to conduct a traffic stop shortly after 5 p.m. March 2 after spotting a gray Nissan being driven erratically.

Authorities said the vehicle fled from officers, leading to a brief pursuit. The Nissan later ran a stop sign and collided with a black Toyota at the intersection of Columbia Street and North Street.

Police said both the driver and passenger of the gray Nissan were apprehended at the scene of the crash.

The female driver of the Toyota and her juvenile daughter were transported to a local hospital for treatment. The driver and passenger of the Nissan were also taken to a hospital.

Officials said all injuries reported in the crash were non-life-threatening.

Charges for the occupants of the gray Nissan are pending as the investigation continues.

item.title: Pascagoula police searching for missing woman last seen near Regency Woods Apartments - Darkhorse Press
item.author: Morgan Howard
item.description:
PASCAGOULA – The Pascagoula Police Department is asking for the public’s help in locating a missing woman who was last seen Monday afternoon.

Police said Kiva Barnes, 46, was last seen around 4 p.m. on March 2, 2026, walking near the Regency Woods Apartments in Pascagoula.

Barnes is described as 5 feet 6 inches tall and weighing about 150 pounds.

Police are asking anyone who may have seen Barnes or who has information about her whereabouts to contact the Pascagoula Police Department at 228-762-2211 or send a message through the department’s social media page.

No additional information was immediately available.

item.title: Let out your inner science nerd: The Fossil Road Show is Saturday - Darkhorse Press
item.author: Special to Darkhorse Press
item.description:
JACKSON – Fossil lovers, rock collectors, and budding paleontologists, get ready to pack up your fossils and hit the road!

The Mississippi Department of Wildlife, Fisheries, and Parks’ Mississippi Museum of Natural Science proudly presents its 23rd Annual Fossil Road Show on Saturday, March 7, from 10 a.m. to 3 p.m.

This hands‑on, family‑friendly event invites visitors of all ages to bring their fossil finds for on‑the‑spot identification by the museum’s team of expert scientists. Whether you’ve unearthed a curious tooth, a mysterious bone, or a strange imprint in stone, the Fossil Road Show is your chance to discover its ancient story.

A Prehistoric Adventure for Everyone Which Includes

item.title: JSU prepares students for life after college with career expo - Darkhorse Press
item.author: Special to Darkhorse Press
item.description:
By Kelli Sharpe Jackson State University

JACKSON – Jackson State University is helping students get the best return on their college education by finding them employment. JSU hosted the 2026 Spring Career Expo on Feb. 26, at the Walter Payton Center. Over 300 students attended, giving them direct access to representatives from 77 private and public-sector employers.

“Preparing students for life beyond graduation is a responsibility we take seriously at Jackson State University,” said Interim President Denise Jones Gregory. “The Spring Career Expo strengthens the connection between classroom learning and meaningful employment by bringing our students face to face with opportunity.”

In the lead-up to the Spring Career Expo, employer partners were instrumental in preparing students. Representatives from Lockheed Martin, Cooperative Energy, Continental Tires, Regions Bank, Southern Company and Georgia Pacific participated in the Career Expo Prep Day to help students fine-tune their resumes and enhance their interview skills.

“For many of us, this is where the rubber meets the road,” shared Chloe Nash, a senior Psychology major who participated in the prep day. “We invest four years into our education, and now we have to show that we are ready for meaningful employment. Whether in HR or another field, every organization has a place for psychology. I am excited to see where these businesses, connections and opportunities lead me.”

LaShanda Jordan, Ph.D., executive director of Student and Career Services and the JSU Career Service Center, emphasized the significance of these employer partnerships. “The value that our employer partners bring to the preparatory events is immeasurable, as they directly influence the employability of our students. The Career Expo is a vital component of the college experience, providing essential networking opportunities.”

Jordan said 40 students registered for the prep day event to ensure they were well-prepared for the career fair. Additionally, the College of Business helped business majors gain a competitive edge in the job market through a specialized career fair. The Center for Academic and Professional Success (CAPS) sponsored an event just for business students, scheduled ahead of the Spring Career Expo.

“Career readiness events are extremely important to students in the College of Business,” said Shelia Porterfield, Ph.D, associate dean with the JSU College of Business. “They are designed to help prepare students for their entry into the business world and CAPS does this by engaging students in a host of career readiness activities, including professional development courses.”

The Bureau of Labor and Statistics has cited technology and data, healthcare, business and finance and specialized roles as high-demand jobs for 2026 graduates. These fields include information security analysts, software engineers, medical and health services managers, financial analysts, research analysts, and teachers.

According to ZipRecruiter Economic Research, employers are increasingly requiring four-year degrees, with over 70% of companies requiring them for entry-level positions in 2025. In 2024, it was only 55%.

For more information about the 2026 Spring Career Expo and the JSU Career Services Center, please visit JSU Career Services or call 601.979.2477.

item.title: Greenville Fire Department to host district meeting, training for first responders Saturday - Darkhorse Press
item.author: Morgan Howard
item.description:
GREENVILLE – The Greenville Fire Department will host the Mississippi Fire Chiefs and Firefighters Association Northwest Central District Meeting on Saturday, March 7.

The meeting will begin at 9:30 a.m. at 532 Central Street and is open to all first responders, according to the Greenville Fire Department.

Officials said presidents from both the Mississippi Fire Chiefs Association and the Mississippi Firefighters Association will provide updates during the meeting. The Washington County Emergency Management Agency will also present information related to emergency management and preparedness.

In addition to the meeting, several training opportunities will be offered for firefighters in attendance. Planned sessions include live fire burns, firefighter survival training and extrication training.

Firefighters participating in the hands-on training are asked to bring their full gear.

Lunch will be provided for attendees.

The Greenville Fire Department said the event will provide an opportunity for first responders to receive updates, participate in training and strengthen partnerships across the district.

item.title: Former St. Martin High JROTC Instructor Arrested Under New Child Grooming Law - Darkhorse Press
item.author: Therese Apel
item.description:
JACKSON COUNTY — A former JROTC instructor at St. Martin High School has been arrested and charged under Mississippi’s new child grooming law following allegations of inappropriate conduct with a student, authorities said.

The Jackson County Sheriff’s Department arrested Lawrence Richard Dizon Jr. on March 4 and charged him with grooming of a child. The law, enacted in 2025, was designed as a proactive measure to protect children from inappropriate relationships with sexual predators.

Sheriff John Ledbetter said school administrators contacted investigators on Feb. 5 regarding allegations involving Dizon and a student. During the investigation, authorities discovered approximately 1,200 text messages exchanged between Dizon and the alleged victim.

Dizon was terminated from his position at St. Martin High School following the allegations.

On March 4, Dizon appeared before Keith Miller for a probable cause hearing in Jackson County Circuit Court. After the hearing, he was taken into custody and transported to the Jackson County Sheriff’s Department Adult Detention Center.

Bond was set at $35,000, and the case was bound over to await action by a grand jury.

The investigation remains ongoing. Authorities said no additional details will be released at this time to protect the integrity of the case. Anyone with information is asked to contact the Jackson County Sheriff’s Department Criminal Investigative Division.
item.ns1:encoded:
JACKSON COUNTY — A former JROTC instructor at St. Martin High School has been arrested and charged under Mississippi’s new child grooming law following allegations of inappropriate conduct with a student, authorities said.

The Jackson County Sheriff’s Department arrested Lawrence Richard Dizon Jr. on March 4 and charged him with grooming of a child. The law, enacted in 2025, was designed as a proactive measure to protect children from inappropriate relationships with sexual predators.

Sheriff John Ledbetter said school administrators contacted investigators on Feb. 5 regarding allegations involving Dizon and a student. During the investigation, authorities discovered approximately 1,200 text messages exchanged between Dizon and the alleged victim.

Dizon was terminated from his position at St. Martin High School following the allegations.

On March 4, Dizon appeared before Keith Miller for a probable cause hearing in Jackson County Circuit Court. After the hearing, he was taken into custody and transported to the Jackson County Sheriff’s Department Adult Detention Center.

Bond was set at $35,000, and the case was bound over to await action by a grand jury.

The investigation remains ongoing. Authorities said no additional details will be released at this time to protect the integrity of the case. Anyone with information is asked to contact the Jackson County Sheriff’s Department Criminal Investigative Division.

item.title: Columbus police looking for armed suspect in aggravated domestic violence case - Darkhorse Press
item.author: Morgan Howard
item.description:
COLUMBUS – Columbus police are searching for a man wanted on an aggravated domestic violence charge and are warning the public that he is considered armed and dangerous.

According to the Columbus Police Department, a female victim reported at approximately 8:09 a.m. March 3 that she had been physically assaulted by her boyfriend during a domestic dispute.

Police said a warrant has been issued for Deandre Dawan Stancil on a charge of aggravated domestic violence.

Authorities said Stancil is considered armed and dangerous. The investigation remains ongoing.

Anyone with information is asked to contact the Columbus Police Department or Golden Triangle Crime Stoppers.

item.title: UPDATE: Burn Bans Issued in at least 15 Mississippi Counties; Violations Carry Fines - Darkhorse Press
item.author: Therese Apel
item.description:
JACKSON — Burn bans are now in effect in at least 15 Mississippi counties as dry conditions increase wildfire risk, according to the Mississippi Forestry Commission.

The bans prohibit outdoor burning of any kind and will remain in place through various dates in March and early April. County boards of supervisors request burn bans, and the Forestry Commission approves them. All burn bans expire at midnight on the listed end date.

Counties currently under burn bans include:

A burn ban means no outdoor burning of any kind. Anything with an open flame that produces an ember is prohibited, including campfires, bonfires, fire pits, fire rings, burn barrels, debris burning and field burning. Officials warn that wind can carry embers up to a half-mile away, potentially igniting spot fires.

Allowed during a burn ban are propane or gas grills and heaters, as well as charcoal grills, if used according to manufacturer guidelines, away from combustible materials and never left unattended. Officials stress that charcoal briquettes should be completely cooled and doused with water before disposal in a metal container, with ash cold to the touch.

Each burn ban lists whether exemptions apply. Exemptions may include certified burn managers, county fire services, certain commercial contractors with heavy construction equipment that meet Mississippi Department of Environmental Quality regulations, and agricultural field burns. Some counties list no exemptions.

Local sheriff’s departments enforce burn bans. Anyone who knowingly and willfully violates a burn ban is guilty of a misdemeanor and may face a fine ranging from $100 to $500.

item.title: Tuesday Weather: Foggy AM, Spring-like temps - Darkhorse Press
item.author: Mary Apel
item.description:
Areas of fog, some which could be locally dense, are possible through this morning. Use low beam headlights, allow more distance between vehicles, and allow for longer than usual travel times.

Look for high temps in the upper 70s and lower 80s, with partly cloudy skies and pretty dry conditions.

item.title: STEPPING UP TO SERVE: Baptist Healthplex Members Give Generous Donation to CARA - Darkhorse Press
item.author: Morgan Howard
item.description:
CLINTON — What felt like Christmas in March arrived this week at Community Animal Rescue & Adoption.

Members of the Baptist Healthplex in Clinton organized a donation drive to support the animals at CARA, delivering a large collection of supplies for the shelter’s dogs and cats.

Shelter officials said the outpouring of generosity left staff and volunteers overwhelmed with gratitude. While a photo captured a portion of the donated items, CARA representatives said it did not reflect the full scope of what was given.

“We are absolutely blown away by their generosity,” CARA said in a statement. “There is no way to convey how incredible it feels to know how much our community cares and supports our mission and the animals we care for here.”

CARA expressed appreciation for the Healthplex members who contributed, calling the effort humbling and deeply meaningful.

The shelter relies heavily on community support to provide food, medical care and safe housing for animals awaiting adoption. Officials said acts of kindness like this help ensure the organization can continue its mission of rescuing, rehabilitating and rehoming animals in need.

CARA extended a heartfelt thank-you to everyone who participated in the drive, noting that the generosity will make a direct impact on the lives of the animals in its care.

item.title: Silver Alert issued for missing 35-year-old man with paranoid schizophrenia in Jackson - Darkhorse Press
item.author: Morgan Howard
item.description:
JACKSON – Authorities have issued a Silver Alert for a 35-year-old man reported missing from a personal care home in Jackson.

Larry Layton, 35, was last seen at St. David’s Personal Care Home in the 3000 block of Capitol Street. Officials said he walked away from the facility.

Layton is described as 6 feet 1 inch tall and weighing approximately 230 pounds. His clothing description at the time of his disappearance is unknown.

Authorities said Layton has been diagnosed with paranoid schizophrenia.

Anyone with information on Layton’s whereabouts is urged to contact local law enforcement immediately.

item.title: Silver Alert issued for 63-year-old Jackson man - Darkhorse Press
item.author: Therese Apel
item.description:
JACKSON — The Mississippi Bureau of Investigation has activated a Silver Alert for a 63-year-old Jackson man who was last seen Thursday morning.

Greg King was last seen about 10:30 a.m. Feb. 26 in the 1600 block of Westhaven Boulevard in Jackson, according to the Mississippi Bureau of Investigation. Authorities said he was walking in an unknown direction at the time.

King is 5 feet 5 inches tall, weighing approximately 140 pounds, with brown hair and blue eyes.

Family members told authorities that King suffers from a medical condition that may impair his judgment.

The Silver Alert was issued on behalf of the Jackson Police Department.

Anyone with information about King’s whereabouts is asked to contact the Jackson Police Department or local law enforcement immediately.

item.title: Ridgeland Police Launch ‘Cover the Cruiser’ Fundraiser for Special Olympics Mississippi - Darkhorse Press
item.author: Therese Apel
item.description:
RIDGELAND — The Ridgeland Police Department has kicked off its annual “Cover the Cruiser” fundraiser to benefit Special Olympics Mississippi, inviting residents to donate and sign their names on a patrol vehicle in support of local athletes.

The fundraiser runs through May 1 at participating locations around the city. Community members can stop by, make a donation of any amount and add their signature to a marked patrol car as part of the campaign. All proceeds go directly to programs that help Special Olympics athletes train, compete and succeed.

“No donation is too small — and every signature makes a difference,” the department said in announcing the event, encouraging residents to show their support for athletes across the state.

According to Special Olympics’ website, the organization is a global sports movement that works to unleash the human spirit through the transformative power and joy of sport. Through programming in sports, health, education and community building, Special Olympics addresses inactivity, stigma, isolation and injustice faced by people with intellectual disabilities.

The organization provides high-quality training and competition in an inclusive culture through Unified Sports, which allows individuals with and without intellectual disabilities to compete together. Special Olympics offers more than 30 Olympic-style individual and team sports for athletes ages 2 to 99. Programs also include Unified Schools initiatives and Young Athletes, which helps children as young as 2 develop basic skills.

The Ridgeland Police Department’s fundraiser is part of ongoing efforts by law enforcement agencies nationwide to support Special Olympics through community-based events.

Residents can watch for the marked patrol vehicle at locations throughout Ridgeland through May 1 to participate.

item.title: Police say narcotics kept suspect awake for days before Gulfport attack - Darkhorse Press
item.author: Morgan Howard
item.description:
GULFPORT – A Gulfport man has been arrested and charged after two people were injured in a reported cutting Monday night on Palmer Drive, according to the Gulfport Police Department.

Police said 35-year-old Shaun Andrew Lawler of Gulfport was arrested March 2 and charged with one count of aggravated domestic assault and one count of aggravated assault.

Officers responded around 9:40 p.m. to a report of a cutting in the 2800 block of Palmer Drive. When officers arrived, they found two victims suffering from multiple lacerations, police said. Both victims were transported to a local hospital and were listed in stable condition.

Lawler was located a short time later and taken into custody.

Police said the investigation indicated the incident stemmed from Lawler reportedly being awake for several days due to the use of narcotics.

Lawler was processed and transported to the Harrison County Adult Detention Center, where he is being held on a $400,000 bond.

Anyone with information is asked to contact the Gulfport Police Department at 228-868-5959 or Mississippi Coast Crime Stoppers at 877-787-5898.

item.title: Pedestrian in Wheelchair Killed in Two-Vehicle Crash on Raymond Road - Darkhorse Press
item.author: Therese Apel
item.description:
JACKSON — A pedestrian traveling in a wheelchair was killed Monday evening after being struck by two vehicles on Raymond Road, according to the Jackson Police Department.

Officers responded about 6:40 p.m. to the intersection of Raymond Road and Wilson Boulevard for a reported fatal crash. Police said the pedestrian was hit by a 2012 white Ford Fusion and a 2010 silver Chevrolet Malibu.

According to investigators, the pedestrian was traveling eastbound on Raymond Road in a wheelchair near the center lane when he was struck by the Ford Fusion, which was also traveling east. The impact ejected the victim into the westbound lanes, where he was then struck by the Chevrolet Malibu traveling west in the inside lane.

The victim became trapped underneath the Malibu and was extricated by the Jackson Fire Department. He was pronounced dead at the scene.

Both drivers remained at the scene and were interviewed by responding officers and an accident investigator. Police said each driver was issued a citation for no insurance.

The investigation is ongoing.

item.title: Pearl River County Sheriff's Department looking for missing teen - Darkhorse Press
item.author: Morgan Howard
item.description:
PEARL RIVER COUNTY – Authorities are asking for the public’s assistance in locating a missing 16-year-old who left her residence early Tuesday morning.

According to officials, Starlyn McIntyre left her home at approximately 2 a.m. in her family’s 2013 Chrysler Town and Country van. The vehicle bears Mississippi tag DB4768U.

It is currently unknown where McIntyre may be headed.

Anyone with information regarding her whereabouts is urged to contact Pearl River County Central Dispatch at 601-749-5482 or send a message through the department’s official social media platform.

The case remains active.

item.title: Pascagoula police looking for man wanted for allegedly stealing 10 pair of glasses from Walmart - Darkhorse Press
item.author: Morgan Howard
item.description:
PASCAGOULA – The Pascagoula Police Department is asking for the public’s help in locating a suspect wanted in connection with a felony shoplifting investigation.

Police said Carldarone Williams is suspected of stealing 10 pairs of eyeglasses from the Walmart Vision Center on Feb. 20. Authorities estimate the total value of the items at more than $2,100.

Investigators are requesting assistance from anyone who may know Williams’ whereabouts.

Anyone with information is asked to contact the Pascagoula Police Department at 228-762-2211 or send a message through the department’s official social media page.

item.title: One injured in shooting at West Jackson Mart in Tupelo - Darkhorse Press
item.author: Morgan Howard
item.description:
TUPELO — Tupelo police are investigating a shooting that occurred Tuesday evening at a gas station on West Jackson Street.

According to the Tupelo Police Department, officers responded to a report of a shooting at the West Jackson Mart, located at 1391 W. Jackson St., at the corner of West Jackson Street and Lumpkin Avenue.

Police said one victim was transported to North Mississippi Medical Center with non-life-threatening injuries.

Officers remained on scene as of Tuesday night and were actively investigating the incident. Authorities said there is no known threat to the public at this time and believe the shooting to be an isolated incident.

Additional information will be released as it becomes available.
